Amended Motion To S/A The Settlement And Request A New Trial Date In Court
Off Calendar Jan-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ANNIE CHAN-LANIER Amended Motion to Set Aside the Settlement and Request a New Trial Date In Court. Appearances: Annie Chan-Lanier Plaintiff in Pro Per and moving party. Moving party failed to give notice, pursuant to San Francisco Local Rule 8.3. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Insufficient evidence to support claims of duress or fraud. Stipulation to the agreement was made by the parties personally and in open Court. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-09-2009 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-09-2009 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jun-16-09 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 212
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Court Trial/Settlement Conference. Commissioner William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Lois Jones, CSR #3689.

Paul G. Minoletti, Esq. appeared with plaintiffs James Lanier and Annie Lanier, who were placed under oath and examined.
Edward J. Rodzewich, Esq. appeared for defendants Chiock Dong Wong, Ka Kam Wong and Kathy Lei May Wong, a/k/a Kathy Hernandez.
Notice of Time and Place of Trial was filed on September 4, 2008.
Doe defendants were dismissed today, March 9, 2009.

Terms of the Settlement Agreement were read into the record by Mr. Rodzewich. In accordance with the agreement reached by the parties, defendants Chiock Dong Wong, Ka Kam Wong and Kathy Lei May Wong, a/k/a Kathy Hernandez shall pay plaintiffs James Lanier and Annie Lanier the sum of $90,000.00 in two installments. The first payment of $45,000.00 shall be due in thirty days, and the second payment of $45,000.00 shall be due in sixty days.

If the above-mentioned payment schedule is adhered to by the defendants, plaintiffs will file a dismissal as to all causes of action, and general release as to all claims known and unknown shall issue. Plaintiffs agree to remove their property form the storage pod within sixty days of today's date.

Both James Lanier & Annie Lanier were voir dired by the Court as to the terms and their understanding of the agreement.

Settlement Agreement reached by the parties shall be reduced to writing within three days of today's date by Mr. Rodzewich.

Matter is continued to June 16, 2009 @ 10:00 a.m. in Department 212 on the CRC 3.1385 Dismissal Calendar.
